example_prompt: List available payment channels in Cameroon to offer MTN MoMo and Orange Money to users.

country.description: ISO 3166-1 alpha-2 country code to filter channels by country, e.g. "CM".

amount.description: Filter channels that accept this specific amount.

currency.description: ISO 4217 currency code to filter channels, e.g. "XAF".

name.description: Human-readable channel name, e.g. "MTN Mobile Money".

desc.description: Short description of the channel, e.g. "Pay with MTN MoMo".

active.description: Whether the channel is currently active.

countries.description: List of ISO 3166-1 alpha-2 country codes where this channel is available.

id.description: Unique channel identifier. Same value as slug (e.g. "cm.mtn").

slug.description: Machine-readable channel slug used when initiating payments (e.g. "cm.mtn").

reference.description: Internal identifier in chn.xxx format. Do not confuse with the payment reference.

delivery.description: Delivery information. Null in most cases.

payment_delivery.description: Payment delivery information. Null in most cases.

live.description: Whether this channel is available in live mode.

min_amount.description: Minimum transaction amount accepted by this channel.

max_amount.description: Maximum transaction amount accepted by this channel.

payout.description: Whether this channel supports payouts (1 = yes).

charge.description: Whether this channel supports charges (1 = yes).

collect.description: Whether this channel supports collection (1 = yes).

currency.description: ISO 4217 currency code accepted by this channel.

gotchas: Always filter by country and currency before displaying channels to users — not all channels are available in every country, and showing irrelevant options degrades UX.
Use min_amount and max_amount to validate the user's payment amount before initiating a transaction; submitting an out-of-range amount will result in a rejected payment.
Store the channel slug (not the id or name) when initiating a payment — it is the required identifier in subsequent API calls.
Cache the response — channel data changes rarely, but min/max amounts can be updated by Notch Pay without notice. A daily refresh is sufficient.
The Authorization header uses your public key with no 'Bearer ' prefix.
